claimText: Organ-on-a-chip (OoC) is a micro-scale system that mimics the human body environment using 3D culture models.
evidenceExcerpt: Organ-on-a-chip (OoC) refers to a micro-scale system used for mimicking the human body environment. It is a human-relevant 3D culture model
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and structural nature of OoC technology.

claimText: Organ-on-a-chip (OoC) technology is classified as a 'New Approach Method' (NAM).
evidenceExcerpt: It is also known as 'New Approach Methods' (NAMs) and uses 3D culture models.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question linking OoC to NAMs.

claimText: In Organ-on-a-chip systems, cells grow into 3D structures on a chip using a polymer that resembles real tissue.
evidenceExcerpt: Cells grow into 3D structures on a chip using a polymer resembling real tissue.
examUse: Statement-based question on the material composition and cellular growth mechanism of OoC.
distractorHooks: Use of metallic substrates instead of polymers

claimText: Organ-on-a-chip (OoC) technology provides an ethical and cost-effective platform for accurate drug testing and personalized medicine.
evidenceExcerpt: Provides an ethical, cost-effective platform for accurate drug testing and personalized medicine.
examUse: Application-based question on the benefits and practical uses of OoC in healthcare.
distractorHooks: Highly expensive compared to traditional methods
Limited to basic research without clinical application
